summary: Ukraine faced a massive coordinated airstrike on 24‑25 May, with 690 missiles and drones launched and 604 enemy assets downed. Ukrainian special forces successfully struck a Russian fuel‑pipeline hub, igniting an 800 m² fire that threatened major Moscow‑area airports. Russia continued to employ the controversial “Oreshnyk” missile, striking Bila Tserkva and other command sites, while civilian casualties rose in Kyiv and Starobelsk.
key_points: Ukraine’s SBU drone strike destroyed a key Russian fuel‑pipeline transfer station, sparking an 800 m² fire that fed Moscow‑area airports (Source: @UkraineNow/81429).
On 24‑25 May Russia launched 690 air‑launched weapons (90 rockets, 600 Shahed‑type UAVs); Ukrainian air defence shot down 604 targets (55 rockets, 549 UAVs) (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Russian forces used the “Oreshnyk” missile on Bila Tserkva, damaging a garage cooperative and nearby infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/81426).
Ukrainian prosecutors investigate a night‑time attack on Bila Tserkva district, suspecting the same “Oreshnyk” system (Source: @UkraineNow/81413).
A Russian strike on Kyiv killed 2 civilians and injured 56, prompting a state emergency response (Source: @UkraineNow/81414).
More than 60 children were injured during a Ukrainian assault on a college in Starobelsk, according to local officials (Source: @tass_agency/377330).
A residential complex housing the Albanian ambassador was hit in a Russian attack, raising diplomatic concerns (Source: @UkraineNow/81428).
Ukrainian MP reported damage to Zelensky’s and businessman Timur Mindich’s apartments in Kyiv during the night attack (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30398).

sections.executive_summary: Ukraine’s SBU successfully hit a Russian fuel‑pipeline hub, igniting a large fire that threatens Moscow’s airport fuel supplies. Russia unleashed a massive airstrike on 24‑25 May, deploying 690 missiles and drones, of which Ukrainian defences downed 604. The conflict escalated further with Russia’s use of the “Oreshnyk” missile in Bila Tserkva and rising civilian casualties in Kyiv and Starobelsk.

points: SBU‑operated UAVs struck a Russian oil‑pipeline transfer station, causing an 800 m² blaze that could disrupt fuel deliveries to Sheremetyevo, Domodedovo and Vnukovo airports (Source: @UkraineNow/81429).

Ukrainian air defence units intercepted 55 ballistic/ cruise missiles and 549 hostile UAVs during the 24‑25 May combined strike, preventing further damage to critical infrastructure (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Ukrainian prosecutors are examining the night‑time attack on Bila Tserkva district, suspecting the use of the “Oreshnyk” missile system (Source: @UkraineNow/81413).
MP Aleksey Goncharenko confirmed that the apartments of President Zelensky and businessman Timur Mindich were damaged in the Kyiv night attack (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30398).
Ukrainian forces reported a group of UAVs over the Kyiv reservoir heading toward the capital, indicating ongoing reconnaissance and strike attempts (Source: @kpszsu/62638).

The Russian Ministry of Defence acknowledged using the “Oreshnyk” missile against “military command objects” in Bila Tserkva, alongside Iskander, Kinzhal and Zircon missiles (Source: @UkraineNow/81431).

Russia launched a mixed salvo of 1 medium‑range ballistic missile, 2 Kinzhal hypersonic missiles, 3 Zircon anti‑ship missiles, 30 Iskander‑M/S‑400 missiles, 54 cruise missiles (X‑101/Iskander‑K/Caliber) and 600 Shahed‑type UAVs from multiple launch zones (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
A Russian strike hit a residential complex where the Albanian ambassador resides, prompting diplomatic condemnation (Source: @UkraineNow/81428).
Russian forces reported UAV activity over southern Black Sea waters near the settlement of Pivdenne, indicating maritime reconnaissance (Source: @kpszsu/62663).
Russian officials claimed the “Oreshnyk” missile hit a garage cooperative in Bila Tserkva, causing structural damage (Source: @UkraineNow/81426).
Ukrainian sources reported a group of UAVs moving from the north of Chernihiv toward Slavutych, suggesting pressure on the eastern approaches (Source: @kpszsu/62633).
A cluster of UAVs was observed over the Sumy region near Buryn, heading westward, potentially targeting supply lines (Source: @kpszsu/62614).
Ukrainian intelligence noted a reactive UAV passing Olishivka toward the Desna River in Chernihiv oblast (Source: @kpszsu/62649).
Reports indicated a UAV group over the northern Kyiv region, flying above the Kyiv reservoir toward the capital (Source: @kpszsu/62638).
A UAV sortie from Bryansk was tracked heading to Snovsk, likely for reconnaissance or strike missions (Source: @eRadarrua/93506).
Multiple UAV sightings in northern Chernihiv oblast, including a group heading toward Slavutych and another reactive UAV near Desna (Source: @kpszsu/62633, @kpszsu/62649).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV cluster over the northern Kyiv region, crossing the Kyiv water reservoir (Source: @kpszsu/62638).
A UAV launch from Bryansk was observed moving toward Snovsk, indicating northern Russian air activity (Source: @eRadarrua/93506).
Ukrainian air defence recorded downing of several UAVs in the north, contributing to the overall 604 targets neutralised (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
No significant ground troop movements were reported near the Belarusian border, though Russian pressure on Belarus was noted (Source: @UkraineNow/81435).
A UAV was detected over the Black Sea heading toward the settlement of Pivdenne, suggesting southern maritime reconnaissance (Source: @kpszsu/62663).
Russian missile launches from the Kapustin Yar (Astrahan) region included a medium‑range ballistic missile, indicating southern strike capability (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
No direct ground engagements were reported in the southern oblasts during the 24‑25 May air campaign, but air defence activity was high (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Ukrainian authorities noted increased Russian pressure on Belarus, potentially to open a southern flank, though no troop movements were observed (Source: @UkraineNow/81435).
Civilian casualties in Kyiv from the night attack underscore the threat of southern‑origin missile trajectories (Source: @UkraineNow/81414).
Russia launched 90 rockets and 600 Shahed‑type UAVs on 24‑25 May; Ukrainian air defence shot down 55 rockets and 549 UAVs, totaling 604 neutralised targets (Source: @kpszsu/62647).

The missile mix included 1 medium‑range ballistic missile (Kapustin Yar), 2 Kinzhal hypersonic missiles, 3 Zircon anti‑ship missiles, 30 Iskander‑M/S‑400 missiles, and 54 cruise missiles (X‑101/Iskander‑K/Caliber) (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Ukrainian air defence reported successful interceptions over Kyiv, with multiple air‑raid alerts and subsequent clearances on 24 May (Source: @eRadarrua/93456, @eRadarrua/93468).

UAV activity was reported across multiple fronts, including northern Chernihiv, Kyiv reservoir, Black Sea, and Bryansk‑to‑Snovsk routes, indicating widespread Russian drone deployment (Source: @kpszsu/62633, @kpszsu/62638, @kpszsu/62663, @eRadarrua/93506).
Russian use of the “Oreshnyk” missile, described as a system capable of delivering nuclear warheads, marks a significant escalation in missile technology employed (Source: @UkraineNow/81426).

key_developments: Ukraine’s SBU successfully struck a Russian fuel‑pipeline hub, igniting an 800 m² fire that threatens fuel supplies to Moscow’s major airports (Source: @UkraineNow/81429).
Russia launched a massive air campaign on 24‑25 May with 690 missiles and UAVs; Ukrainian defences downed 604 of these, including 55 rockets and 549 drones (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
The controversial “Oreshnyk” missile was used by Russia against Bila Tserkva, damaging civilian infrastructure and prompting international concern (Source: @UkraineNow/81426).
A night‑time strike in Kyiv resulted in 2 civilian deaths and 56 injuries, highlighting the ongoing threat to the capital (Source: @UkraineNow/81414).
More than 60 children were injured during a Ukrainian attack on a college in Starobelsk, underscoring the civilian toll of ground operations (Source: @tass_agency/377330).
A residential complex housing the Albanian ambassador was hit in a Russian attack, raising diplomatic tensions (Source: @UkraineNow/81428).
Ukrainian MP Goncharenko confirmed damage to President Zelensky’s and businessman Timur Mindich’s apartments during the Kyiv night attack (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30398).
sections.threat_assessment: Russia is intensifying its missile and UAV strikes, employing advanced systems like Kinzhal, Iskander, and the newly publicized “Oreshnyk,” which threatens both military and civilian targets. Ukrainian air defences have proven effective, downing a majority of incoming threats, but civilian casualties remain high in Kyiv and contested areas. Continued UAV reconnaissance across all fronts suggests Russia is seeking targeting data for future high‑precision strikes.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine’s intelligence and special forces are focusing on high‑value sabotage, exemplified by the SBU pipeline strike, while bolstering air defence across the north, east, and south. International partners have condemned attacks on diplomatic residences and civilian sites, potentially leading to increased sanctions and aid. Belarus remains a strategic concern, with Russia pressuring it for deeper involvement, though no troop movements have been confirmed.
sections.intelligence_notes: UAV activity is widespread, with clusters observed over Chernihiv, Kyiv reservoir, Black Sea, and from Bryansk toward Snovsk, indicating a coordinated reconnaissance network. The use of the “Oreshnyk” missile may signal a shift toward higher‑payload, longer‑range strike capabilities. Monitoring of Russian launch sites in Kapustin Yar, Astrakhan, and Crimea is critical for early warning of future missile salvos.
